contributions_v_window.v172_727_ci_red_to_green: #727 CI Test FAILED at 23:15:33Z on commit d457ecb — outbox/route.ts flagged by stale-marker test on `\bBIP[_-]?322\b` from docstring inside GET 405 response body. Whoabuddy commented at 23:20Z naming false-positive + recommending stripStringLiterals (option 1 from 3-option fix menu). I had stripStringLiterals coded but wired into boolean helper `getHandlerHasAuthToken()` only — NOT into the structural enforcement test against real files. Two code paths diverged silently. Force-pushed e4506fa with two changes: (1) wire stripStringLiterals into structural test, (2) refactor to single-source-of-truth via `findAuthTokenInGetHandler(content): RegExp | null` — both helper and structural test now route through this one function. v172 lesson: 'verify before publishing' must widen to 'verify EVERY code path against the failure scenario.' Passing helper unit tests insufficient if real-files test uses different scan logic. Single-source-of-truth refactor is the structural answer (eliminate the second code path rather than fix it). 16min from CI red (23:15) to fix pushed (23:31).
